sections.label: Section 1.1180
sections.translation: I am called blessed Agave in the revels .

sections.label: Section 1.1181
sections.translation: Who else ?

sections.label: Section 1.1181b
sections.translation: Kadmos ’—

sections.label: Section 1.1182
sections.translation: Kadmos ’ what ?

sections.label: Section 1.1182b
sections.translation: His other offspring took hold of this beast after me , after me . This is a lucky catch !

sections.label: Section 1.1184
sections.translation: Share in the feast then .

sections.label: Section 1.1184b
sections.translation: What ? I share in the feast , wretched woman ?

sections.label: Section 1.1185
sections.translation: The bull is young ; his cheek is just growing downy under his soft-haired crest .

sections.label: Section 1.1188
sections.original: πρέπει γʼ ὥστε θὴρ ἄγραυλος φόβῃ .
sections.translation: Yes , his hair looks like a wild beast ’ s .

sections.label: Section 1.1189
sections.translation: Bacchus , a wise huntsman ,

sections.label: Section 1.1190
sections.translation: wisely set the Maenads against this beast .

sections.label: Section 1.1192
sections.translation: Our lord is a hunter .

sections.label: Section 1.1193
sections.translation: Do you praise me ?

sections.label: Section 1.1193b
sections.translation: I praise you .

sections.label: Section 1.1194
sections.translation: Soon the Kadmeans —

sections.label: Section 1.1195
sections.translation: And your son Pentheus , too —

sections.label: Section 1.1195b
sections.translation: Will praise his mother who has caught this lion-like prey .

sections.label: Section 1.1197
sections.translation: Extraordinary .

sections.label: Section 1.1197b
sections.translation: And extraordinarily caught .

sections.label: Section 1.1198
sections.translation: Are you proud ?

sections.label: Section 1.1198b
sections.translation: I am delighted , for I have performed great — yes , great — and notable deeds on this hunt .

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original: δεῖξόν νυν , ὦ τάλαινα , σὴν νικηφόρον
sections.translation: Now show the citizens , wretched woman , the booty which you have brought in victory .

sections.label: Section 1202
sections.translation: You who dwell in this fair-towered city of the Theban land , come to see this prey which we the daughters of Kadmos hunted down ,

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.translation: not with thonged Thessalian javelins , or with nets , but with the fingers of our white arms . And then should huntsmen boast and use in vain the work of spear-makers ? But we caught and

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.translation: tore apart the limbs of this beast with our very own hands . Where is my old father ? Let him approach . And where is my son Pentheus ? Let him take a ladder and raise its steps against the house so that he can fasten to the triglyphs this

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.translation: lion ’ s head which I have captured and brought here .

sections.label: Section 1216
sections.translation: Follow me , carrying the miserable burden of Pentheus , follow me , slaves , before the house ; exhausted from countless searches , I am bringing his body , for I discovered it in the folds of Kithairon ,

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.translation: torn apart ; I picked up nothing in the same place , and it was lying in the woods where discovery was difficult . For some one told me of my daughters ’ bold deeds , when I had already come within the walls of the city on my return from the Bacchae with old Teiresias .

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: πάλιν δὲ κάμψας εἰς ὄρος κομίζομαι
sections.translation: I turned back to the mountain and now bring here my child who was killed by the Maenads . For I saw Autonoe , who once bore Actaeon to Aristaeus , and Ino with her , still mad in the thicket , wretched creatures .

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.original: τὴν δʼ εἶπέ τίς μοι δεῦρο βακχείῳ ποδὶ
sections.translation: But some one told me that Agave was coming here with Bacchic foot , and this was correct , for I see her — no happy sight !

sections.label: Section 1233
sections.translation: Father , you may make a great boast , that you have born daughters the best by far of all

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.original: θνητῶν · ἁπάσας εἶπον , ἐξόχως δʼ ἐμέ ,
sections.translation: mortals . I mean all of us , but myself especially , who have left my shuttle at the loom and gone on to greater things , to catch wild animals with my two hands . And having taken him , I carry these spoils of honor in my arms , as you see ,

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.original: ὡς ἀγκρεμασθῇ · σὺ δέ , πάτερ , δέξαι χεροῖν ·
sections.translation: so that they may hang from your house . You father , receive them in your hands . Preening yourself in my catch , call your friends to a feast . For you are blessed , blessed , now that we have performed these deeds .

sections.label: Section 1244
sections.original: ὦ πένθος οὐ μετρητὸν οὐδʼ οἷόν τʼ ἰδεῖν ,
sections.translation: O grief beyond measuring , one which I cannot stand to see ,

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.translation: that you have performed murder with miserable hands . Having cast down a fine sacrificial victim to the gods , you invite Thebes and me to a banquet . Alas , first for your troubles , then for my own . How justly , yet too severely ,

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.translation: lord Bromius the god has destroyed us , though he is a member of our own family .

sections.label: Section 1251
sections.original: ὡς δύσκολον τὸ γῆρας ἀνθρώποις ἔφυ
sections.translation: How morose and sullen in its countenance is man ’ s old age ! I hope that my son is a good hunter , taking after his mother ’ s ways , when he goes after wild beasts

sections.label: Section 1255
sections.translation: together with the young men of Thebes . But all he can do is fight with the gods . You must admonish him , father . Who will call him here to my sight , so that he may see how lucky I am ?

sections.label: Section 1259
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ · φρονήσασαι μὲν οἷʼ ἐδράσατε
sections.translation: Alas , alas ! When you realize what you have done

sections.label: Section 1260
sections.original: ἀλγήσετʼ ἄλγος δεινόν · εἰ δὲ διὰ τέλους
sections.translation: you will suffer a terrible pain . But if you remain forever in the state you are in now , though hardly fortunate , you will not imagine that you are unfortunate .

sections.label: Section 1263
sections.original: τί δʼ οὐ καλῶς τῶνδʼ ἢ τί λυπηρῶς ἔχει ;
sections.translation: But what of these matters is not right , or what is painful ?

sections.label: Section 1264
sections.original: πρῶτον μὲν ἐς τόνδʼ αἰθέρʼ ὄμμα σὸν μέθες .
sections.translation: First cast your eye up to this sky .

sections.label: Section 1265
sections.original: ἰδού · τί μοι τόνδʼ ἐξυπεῖπας εἰσορᾶν ;
sections.translation: All right ; why do you tell me to look at it ?

sections.label: Section 1266
sections.original: ἔθʼ αὑτὸς ἤ σοι μεταβολὰς ἔχειν δοκεῖ ;
sections.translation: Is it still the same , or does it appear to have changed ?

sections.label: Section 1267
sections.translation: It is brighter than before and more translucent .

sections.label: Section 1268
sections.original: τὸ δὲ πτοηθὲν τόδʼ ἔτι σῇ ψυχῇ πάρα ;
sections.translation: Is your soul still quivering ?

sections.label: Section 1269
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα τοὔπος τοῦτο . γίγνομαι δέ πως
sections.translation: I don ’ t understand your words . I have become somehow

sections.label: Section 1270
sections.translation: sobered , changing from my former state of mind .

sections.label: Section 1271
sections.original: κλύοις ἂν οὖν τι κἀποκρίναιʼ ἂν σαφῶς ;
sections.translation: Can you hear and respond clearly ?

sections.label: Section 1272
sections.original: ὡς ἐκλέλησμαί γʼ ἃ πάρος εἴπομεν , πάτερ .
sections.translation: Yes , for I forget what we said before , father .

sections.label: Section 1273
sections.original: ἐς ποῖον ἦλθες οἶκον ὑμεναίων μέτα ;
sections.translation: To whose house did you come in marriage ?

sections.label: Section 1274
sections.original: Σπαρτῷ μʼ ἔδωκας , ὡς λέγουσʼ , Ἐχίονι .
sections.translation: You gave me , as they say , to Echion , the sown man .

sections.label: Section 1275
sections.original: τίς οὖν ἐν οἴκοις παῖς ἐγένετο σῷ πόσει ;
sections.translation: What son did you bear to your husband in the house ?

sections.label: Section 1276
sections.original: Πενθεύς , ἐμῇ τε καὶ πατρὸς κοινωνίᾳ .
sections.translation: Pentheus , from my union with his father .

sections.label: Section 1277
sections.original: τίνος πρόσωπον δῆτʼ ἐν ἀγκάλαις ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: Whose head do you hold in your hands ?

sections.label: Section 1278
sections.original: λέοντος , ὥς γʼ ἔφασκον αἱ θηρώμεναι .
sections.translation: A lion ’ s , as they who hunted him down said .

sections.label: Section 1279
sections.original: σκέψαι νυν ὀρθῶς · βραχὺς ὁ μόχθος εἰσιδεῖν .
sections.translation: Examine it correctly then ; it takes but little effort to see .

sections.label: Section 1280
sections.original: ἔα , τί λεύσσω ; τί φέρομαι τόδʼ ἐν χεροῖν ;
sections.translation: Ah ! What do I see ? What is this that I carry in my hands ?

sections.label: Section 1281
sections.translation: Look at it and learn more clearly .
